description_long: This is a single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a streamlined metallic fuselage and a closed cockpit situated near the front. The aircraft is painted in a matte gray finish, adorned with red stars and red striping on the wings and tail, suggesting a Soviet or Russian military design. The wings are straight and slightly tapered, with no visible winglets, and the tail features a conventional vertical stabilizer with horizontal stabilizers. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the engine cowling is rounded and robust. The landing gear is not visible, implying it may be retractable or not modeled. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century fighter aircraft.
description: A gray, single-propeller military airplane with red star insignias, low straight wings, and a closed cockpit near the nose.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, red star insignias on the wings and fuselage, and the cockpit placement.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the streamlined fuselage, nose-mounted propeller, and the closed cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: Rear oblique view displaying the tail section, red striping on the tail surfaces, and the wing dihedral.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view emphasizing the underwing structure, tail design, and the overall silhouette of the airplane.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight, slightly tapered wings and no winglets; moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable or not modeled.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
